Title: Cloning of wheat keto-acyl thiolase 2B reveals a role of jasmonic acid in grain weight determination

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA-associated developmental regulation linked to organ growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is positively associated with JA status and may contribute to increased cell number, leaf area, and indirectly grain weight
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in mutant and overexpression lines; exogenous JA treatment; JA inhibitor DIEC treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in seedling leaves; downregulated in tgw1 and upregulated by JA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10 μM JA treatment; 200 μM DIEC treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests CNR6 is JA-inducible and may positively regulate cell number in wheat, but direct genetic proof for its role in grain weight is not provided
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible secondary target for improving source capacity, but requires direct genetic validation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNR6 expression is significantly decreased in the low-JA tgw1 mutant. 2. Exogenous JA induces CNR6 expression, whereas the JA inhibitor DIEC suppresses it. 3. Increased CNR6 expression in KAT-2B overexpression plants correlates with larger leaf area, suggesting a role downstream of JA in source development.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher SAG3 expression is associated with increased ABA, early senescence, reduced chlorophyll content, and indirectly lower grain weight in tgw1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in mutant; exogenous ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in young seedling leaves; elevated in tgw1 and induced by ABA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression linked to leaf senescence and chlorophyll reduction
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SAG3 was analyzed as a downstream marker linking elevated ABA in tgw1 to reduced chlorophyll rather than as a primary causal metabolic gene
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a diagnostic marker of ABA-related senescence, not yet a validated engineering target in this study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAG3 expression is increased in tgw1, where ABA accumulates. 2. Exogenous ABA induces SAG3 expression in seedlings. 3. The study proposes that elevated SAG3 marks early senescence contributing to reduced chlorophyll and grain-filling source capacity in the mutant.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Peroxisomal β-oxidation module of JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; supported as essential step for JA production in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates grain development, leaf development, pollen development, and contributes to grain weight determination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid accumulation and increases grain width, grain weight, thousand kernel weight, and yield; loss-of-function reduces JA and grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates in β-oxidation; acetoacetyl-CoA and CoA in the in vitro assay
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Shortened acyl-CoA plus acetyl-CoA; supports JA biosynthetic flux
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes thiolytic cleavage in peroxisomal β-oxidation to shorten acyl-CoA carbon chains and release acetyl-CoA, thereby supporting jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies required β-oxidation step for JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping/BSA; transgenic overexpression in wheat; complementation of tgw1; recombinant enzyme assay; subcellular localization; Arabidopsis kat2 complementation; metabolite profiling of JA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in most tissues except embryo; expression higher than KAT-2A across multiple tissues and developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in most tissues except embryo
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA673987; public microarray data GSE12508; wheat expression database in Triticeae Multi-omics Center
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for wheat overexpression; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for GFP localization construct
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials in 2019 and 2020; pathogen treatment increased JA in one OE line; JA, DIEC, and ABA treatments used for downstream interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression slightly reduced grain number per spike in field trials, indicating a grain number–grain weight trade-off
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The tgw1 allele carries a premature stop mutation (Q364*) predicted to remove two catalytic residues near the C-terminus; KAT-2A likely provides residual protein in mutant; wheat KAT-2A/B rescue Arabidopsis kat2 on sucrose-free medium
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for wheat improvement; overexpression increased TKW and yield, and precise editing/allele optimization of KAT-2B or JA biosynthesis was proposed for breeding
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tgw1 mutant with reduced grain weight and ~50% lower JA carries a premature stop mutation in KAT-2B. 2. KAT-2B encodes an active peroxisomal thiolase in β-oxidation and is required for JA biosynthesis. 3. Overexpression of KAT-2B elevates JA, increases leaf area/chlorophyll, raises grain width and grain weight, and improves yield in field trials. 4. Wild-type KAT-2B complements tgw1 and rescues Arabidopsis kat2 lethality on sucrose-free medium, confirming gene identity and conserved function.
